right_now_section_title: Top 5 LA Food Trends Right Now

right_now.definition: Burgers featuring thick, juicy patties, often with gourmet toppings.
right_now.evidence_of_spread: Multiple establishments across Los Angeles are offering variations of thick burgers, indicating a city-wide trend.
right_now.why_hot: The shift from thin to thick burgers offers a richer, more satisfying flavor profile, appealing to burger enthusiasts seeking a hearty experience.
right_now.go_now_recommendation: Visit Dunsmoor's back bar for a standout thick burger experience.

right_now.definition: Japanese-style pubs offering a variety of small, shareable dishes alongside drinks.
right_now.evidence_of_spread: The proliferation of izakayas in Los Angeles, including new openings in Atwater Village and McCall's expansion, showcases the trend's growth.
right_now.why_hot: Izakayas provide a casual dining experience with diverse menu options, catering to the growing demand for communal and relaxed dining settings.
right_now.go_now_recommendation: Explore the new izakayas in Atwater Village for an authentic Japanese pub experience.

right_now.definition: Innovative takes on traditional bagels, incorporating unique flavors and fillings.
right_now.evidence_of_spread: Chefs at Smögen and Red Room are offering creative bagel options, indicating a broader trend of bagel innovation.
right_now.why_hot: The fusion of traditional bagels with modern flavors appeals to diners seeking new culinary experiences.
right_now.go_now_recommendation: Try the reimagined bagels at Smögen for a fresh twist on a classic favorite.

right_now.definition: Breakfast burritos finished with chili crisp or chili oil for heat and crunch, showing up beyond traditional Mexican spots.
right_now.evidence_of_spread: Seen across multiple LA spots: Eastside breakfast counters, Westside brunch cafes
right_now.why_hot: Chili crisp crossed into breakfast as diners want savory heat early in the day.

right_now.definition: Long-ferment dough bagels with chew and blister—often sourdough or hybrid—sold at specialty bakeries and weekend pop-ups.
right_now.evidence_of_spread: Seen across multiple LA spots: Neighborhood bakehouses, Farmers market bagel stalls
right_now.why_hot: LA’s bakery wave pushed craft bagels with serious fermentation and texture.

about_to_hit.why_it_could_pop: The shift from thin to thick burgers offers a richer, more satisfying flavor profile, appealing to burger enthusiasts seeking a hearty experience.
